analysis.consistency: High. The spell components, casting time, and effects are internally consistent. The description clearly defines the creation, limitations (half HP, no level gain, limited utility), maintenance (repair cost), and termination conditions.
analysis.quality: High. This is a well-detailed and powerful utility/combat spell. The resource cost (1500 gp and 12 hours) is significant, appropriately balancing the effect of gaining a semi-permanent, autonomous copy of a creature. The specific material component detail is robust.

mechanics.type: Illusion/Conjuration (though described as partially real, mechanically it functions as a summoned/created entity)
limitations: Instantly destroyed if the caster casts the spell again.
strengths: Acts as a normal creature (can take actions).
Repairable via substantial cost (100 gp per HP).

analysis.notes: The mechanical distinction between 'illusory duplicate' and 'partially real' formed from ice/snow is important for defining its durability (HP mechanic) while maintaining its visual/behavioral faithfulness to the original. The 12-hour casting time prevents casual combat use.
